full_content:
A UserPromptSubmit hook MUST classify each owner message and force 3-option clarification when a requirement candidate is detected (LLM classification + retrieval-augmented options)

A UserPromptSubmit hook (or peer hook) MUST run on every owner message submitted in chat, and MUST perform the following classification + clarification flow:

CLASSIFICATION (binding):
- The hook MUST invoke a lightweight LLM classifier (Haiku-class or smaller) on each owner message.
- The classifier MUST return exactly one label from the enumeration: { requirement_candidate, clarification, decision, chat }.
- The classifier MUST be prompt-engineered to recognize requirement-language in all forms: imperative ("must", "should", "shall"), descriptive ("X lives in Y", "X is part of Z"), structural (paths, repos, file locations), comparative ("not part of", "separate from", "instead of"), inferred (from screenshots, terse confirmations, multi-paragraph explanations).
- The classifier output MUST be visible to the AI agent as additionalContext (per Claude Code hook contract) so the agent can branch behavior on classification.

REQUIREMENT_CANDIDATE PATH (binding):
When classification == "requirement_candidate", the hook MUST cause the AI agent to:

  STEP 1 — Surface to owner (per GOV-SPEC-CAPTURE-TRANSPARENCY-001):
    The agent MUST surface the classification to the owner in chat at the moment of detection: "I classified your message as a requirement candidate. Here's how I'm interpreting it: [interpretation]. Three likely clarifications are below." The agent MUST NOT proceed with implementation until clarification is received.

  STEP 2 — Build clarification options via tiered retrieval:
    The agent MUST perform a 3-tier search for relevant prior context:
      (a) Deliberation Archive (semantic + structured query on session_id, spec_id, work_item_id, source_type, content keywords)
      (b) Recent chat thread (last N turns of the current session transcript, where N is configurable but defaults to 50)
      (c) Loyal Opposition advisory reports under `independent-progress-assessments/CODEX-INSIGHT-DROPBOX/`
    The agent MUST then call the LLM to generate exactly THREE distinct clarification options, each:
      - concrete (specifies the operational consequence of choosing it)
      - mutually exclusive with the other two where possible
      - annotated with which retrieval source(s) informed it
      - prefixed with a stable label (Option A / B / C)

  STEP 3 — Present via AskUserQuestion (per feedback_use_askuserquestion_for_all_decisions.md):
    The agent MUST present the 3 options via AskUserQuestion. The AskUserQuestion form MUST include:
      - the original requirement statement (verbatim)
      - the 3 options with descriptions
      - an "Other" option (auto-provided by AskUserQuestion)
    The agent MUST NOT proceed with implementation until the owner selects an option.

  STEP 4 — Capture as DELIB:
    On owner selection, the agent MUST archive the requirement + chosen clarification as a DELIB (per DELIB-S330-SPEC-CAPTURE-TRANSPARENCY's flow), with the verbatim owner statement as the DELIB content.

OTHER CLASSIFICATIONS (binding):
- classification == "decision": The agent MUST surface the decision detection per GOV-SPEC-CAPTURE-TRANSPARENCY-001 (positive path), capture as a DELIB if it's a non-trivial decision affecting future sessions, and continue normally.
- classification == "clarification": The agent MUST treat the message as a response to a previously-asked question. If no question is pending, the agent MUST flag this as ambiguous and ask for context.
- classification == "chat": The agent MUST surface the decline-to-capture decision per GOV-SPEC-CAPTURE-TRANSPARENCY-001 (negative path) and continue normally.

ESCAPE HATCH (binding):
- If the owner's requirement statement is unambiguous from chat context alone (e.g., the owner explicitly prescribes a single interpretation with no plausible ambiguity), the agent MAY skip the 3-option ceremony and instead present a single proposed interpretation for approve/reject via AskUserQuestion.
- The agent MUST justify the escape-hatch use in chat (e.g., "this requirement is unambiguous; presenting a single interpretation rather than 3 options").
- The escape hatch MUST NOT be used to silently capture; the owner ALWAYS sees the proposed interpretation before it's archived.

ENFORCEMENT (cross-references):
- This GOV will be registered as a cross-cutting requirement under GOV-CROSS-CUTTING-REQUIREMENTS-MECHANICAL-ENFORCEMENT-001 once the registry is populated.
- The hook implementation contract is specified in DCL-REQUIREMENTS-COLLECTION-HOOK-CONTRACT-001 (companion DCL).
- Implementation work is tracked in IPR-REQUIREMENTS-COLLECTION-HOOK-001 (forthcoming) and a new bridge thread for the hook implementation.

Rationale:
The Slice 8.6 incident (S330, 2026-05-04) exposed two failures:
(1) Prime Builder did not capture the owner's verbatim project-root-boundary topology choice as a DELIB during ISOLATION-017 planning, even though the choice was stated;
(2) The captured planning DELIBs (DELIB-0877/0878/0879) presented three options but never recorded the owner's specific selection.
Had this hook existed, the topology requirement would have been classified as `requirement_candidate`, surfaced to the owner with 3 retrieval-informed clarification options, the owner's choice captured as a DELIB at the moment of decision, and the entire multi-session Slice 8.6 misrouting prevented.

Owner approval: Approve as drafted (S330 chat, 2026-05-04, via AskUserQuestion). Per owner clarifications: detection scope = Option C (LLM-classified); sourcing strategy = Option C (tiered: retrieval-augmented first, fall back to pure LLM).
explicit_change_request: Owner approved insertion via AskUserQuestion: "Approve as drafted". This artifact spawns from DELIB-S330-REQUIREMENTS-COLLECTION-HOOK-WITH-3-OPTION-CLARIFICATION. Source intent: We need a hook that fires to collect requirements and forces the AI agent to present three options for clarification and elaboration of the requirement, reflecting the three most likely clarifications based on the prior deliberation and chat thread or advisory report.
change_reason: S330 spawn-queue item: GOV-REQUIREMENTS-COLLECTION-HOOK-001 created from DELIB-S330-REQUIREMENTS-COLLECTION-HOOK-WITH-3-OPTION-CLARIFICATION per just-approved DELIB-S330-SPEC-CAPTURE-TRANSPARENCY transparency rule.
